The '''''Broadside''-class cruiser kdb-1''', also known as the '''''Broadside''-class corvette''', was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Capital ship/Legends|capital]] [[Warship/Legends|warship]].

==Characteristics==
The ''Broadside''-class shared a common keel design with the [[Gladiator-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Gladiator''-class Star Destroyer]].<ref name="TFUCG">''[[The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide]]''</ref> Designed for long-range engagements, ships of this class had light armor and unimpressive acceleration and maneuverability. They were armed with missile launchers and two [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]].<ref name="EAW" />

Early models were armed with expensive [[diamond boron missile]]s that could be used to deadly effect against formations of tightly-packed ships. The range of the missiles made them very useful in long-range attacks.<ref name="EAW" /> These weapons were replaced by more economical [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]] in later models, though some of the cruisers still kept their lethal armament.<ref name="Broadside cruiser in the databank" /> The payload was 1,200 missiles.<ref name="TFUCG" />

==History==
The ''Broadside''-class was first produced by [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] in the early days of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. It was conceived as a long-range missile ship, and was based on the ''Gladiator''-class.<ref name="TFUCG" /> The design was stripped of passenger compartments, the hangar bays were turned into torpedo bays and the vessel lost some of its armor. Due to the lack of self-sufficiency, the ''Broadside''-class was redesignated as a "cruiser" instead of a "Star Destroyer".<ref name="TFUCG" />

Kuat engineers also decided that great maneuverability was not necessary for the class,<ref name="Broadside cruiser in the databank" /> thus leaving the Broadside slower than the Gladiator.<ref name="TFUCG" />
This was the cause of its vulnerability to other capital warships, and as a result ships of this class were in practice almost never deployed without an escort.<ref name="Broadside cruiser in the databank" /> However, its missiles proved lethal against fighter squadrons and small space stations.<ref name="EAW" />

[[File:Awesome_thing.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Another view of the Broadside cruiser]]
In addition to its design-problems, a number of Broadsides were stolen or otherwise acquired by the [[Zann Consortium/Legends|Zann Consortium]] during the Civil War.<ref name="Forces of Corruption" />

==Behind the scenes==
This type of ship has many similarities to the ''[[Demolisher]]'', a [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] that appeared in the [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars Droids'']] episode "[[The Pirates of Tarnoonga]]." In ''[[The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide]]'', the two designs were given an in-universe connection.

There is some debate in the online community as to the actual size of the ''Broadside''-class. Concept art made available by the game designers suggested that they envisaged a size of around 50–60 meters in length, comparable to small picket cruisers like the [[Guardian-class light cruiser|''Guardian''-class light cruiser]], but the [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] established the [[canon]]ical size of the vessel to be 500 meters long, placing them within the standard [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] category.
